answer:
Our last team project at school was a group presentation on renewable energy sources. It was a pretty big project, and we had to work together closely to get it done. We had a diverse team with different strengths, which made it both challenging and rewarding.

To start, we had a brainstorming session where everyone threw out ideas. It was like a creative storm, with ideas flying around. We decided to divide the work based on our individual strengths. One team member who was great at research took on gathering data, while another who was good at design worked on the visuals. I focused on writing the script and putting together the presentation.

We used a shared online document to keep everything organized, which made it easy to track changes and see what everyone was working on. It was like having a transparent window into the project. We also had regular check-ins, where we'd update each other on our progress and troubleshoot any issues. It was a bit like a relay race, with each of us passing the baton to the next person.

What I learned about myself was that I'm a lot more adaptable than I thought. There were moments when things didn't go as planned, like when our research findings changed and we had to pivot our approach. I realized I could handle the pressure and adjust quickly. I also discovered that I'm a good communicator, which helped keep our team on track.

Overall, the project taught me the value of teamwork and collaboration. It showed me that when you bring together different skills and perspectives, you can achieve something really impressive. It was a great learning experience, and I feel like I grew a lot, both as a student and as a team player.
title: Tell me about your last team project at school. How did everyone work together? What did you learn about yourself?
